And so in various perambulations, the path, I don't know, third of the room probably, and half of them to some significant change in their income has been skill, process, method, combination thereof, mostly in a niche, a business niche, then packaged somehow and turned around and sold to others in that business niche, not the exclusive path, because you have, hey, you two guys. You guys ever sell cars that have been in a car dealership? No. Okay, so you have the aberrant path of generisists, if you will, who go into a niche in which they have never been and make it work. So you've got the guy and little guy there with car dealerships. I pack just barely, right, so windshield repair. Now Guru to auto repair shops, that's a pretty tenuous reach at best, but I mean it's there. I don't know, anybody else maybe. But the most common is, I figured out how to do really well has X. Now I will package up what I know about X and sell it to everybody else who does X. And look, it existed before me, but it's certainly a model that we can give me credit for institutionalizing and perfecting, and it's been a path to big increases for a lot of people. Some, like two in financial services, have the added benefit of their information marketing consumer also then affiliates with their broker dealer or sells their investments or whatever, so they really turn their information marketing customer into a distributor network sales force of some kind as well. But you ought not, if you're in the two thirds of the room roughly who are not playing this game, you ought to look at it carefully. The niche model for most is superior to a big broad general market for a long list of reasons we won't take time to talk about here, but one goes back to this big fish in a small pond kind of thing. The caution I would give you is that about the whole idea of moving into info marketing, the big caution I would give you is that a lot of people want to run away from where they already are because they're tired of it, they're bored with it, and they think everybody in their business is either cheap or an idiot, and so they want to go deal with somebody else. I was reading, I read a chapter I think last night, Newman's biography in some interviewer asked him why he got so involved in auto racing and he says because I can't stand hanging around with actors. So there's a tendency to have been a very, very successful restaurant owner, but the last person on earth I want to sell to is restaurant owners. With the lawyers, this is very understandable, but with everybody else, more often than not it's a mistake because you're sacrificing so much on the asset side of the ledger, affinity, understanding, some level of credibility and so forth. The niche that you're in in your core business is often the niche you should be in or that you should start with in your information marketing business, but by and large it's hard to ignore this particular type of opportunity if you have figured out how to be successful doing just about anything because then there's a whole bunch of other people who will pay one way or another to find out what it is that you know. The last quick thing I would say about it is that the other thing that I believe discourages some people from going down this path is that they see a lot of information marketers already in their niche and they think saturation. In almost every instance, it's of little or no legitimacy, has an obstacle.
